# masonletter

The complete AI context repository for **Mason** — a twice-weekly newsletter for operators building AI systems that actually run in production.

Published Monday and Thursday. Written for heads of operations, COOs, and founders at 20 to 500 person companies who want to build with AI, not feel behind because of it.

Mason is published by **Quarry**, an AI infrastructure consultancy that builds custom systems for operators.

**This repo is the AI's brain.** Every Mason issue is generated by loading these files as context into Claude. The documents here are authoritative.

---

## Repository Contents

```
masonletter/
├── README.md                                    ← You are here
├── mason-brand-bible-v3.md                      ← Load first. Every word written under the Mason name.
├── mason-day-type-definitions-v2.md             ← What qualifies as Monday's Build or Thursday's Move.
├── mason-voice-and-emotion.md                   ← HOW Mason sounds. Opening and closing patterns.
├── mason-finalization-agent-prompt-v5.md        ← Master prompt for the writing AI. Current.
└── mason-newsletter-template-v5.html            ← HTML email template with visual vocabulary.
```

---

## The Two Brand Promises

Every Mason issue keeps two promises:

1. **Calm.** The reader leaves less anxious than they arrived.
2. **Substance.** The reader leaves with one real concrete thing they didn't know before.

If an issue fails either promise, it doesn't ship. Skip the day instead.

---

## The One Sentence

Every issue is a stress test of:

> *"You're not behind. You just haven't been shown how to build."*

---

## The Publication Schedule

Mason publishes twice per week:

- **Monday — THE BUILD** (1,500 to 2,200 words)
  A real build, broken down. Case studies, public engineering writeups, or Quarry portfolio engagements. Authority-demonstrating.

- **Thursday — THE MOVE** (1,200 to 1,800 words)
  A specific tactical thing the reader can apply this week. Prompt patterns, workflows, decision frameworks, small systems.

Tuesday, Wednesday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday — nothing. Deliberate.

---

## The Visual Vocabulary

Every Mason issue includes at least one diagram. This is non-negotiable — it's Mason's visual signature.

Mason uses a 5-type diagram system:

1. **System Flow** — architectures (data sources → AI layer → output)
2. **Time Collapse** — before/after efficiency gains
3. **The Stack** — tool/model layers used in the build
4. **Cost Curve** — cost/time/headcount drops
5. **Big Number Comparison** — stark two-value contrasts

Every issue also has:
- **The Benchmark** — a hero number anchoring the story at the top
- **A source block quote** — direct from the article, full-bleed clay background
- **A pull quote** — one sentence from Mason's writing that earns pullout
- **The Receipts** (Build days only) — outcome numbers on a black block
- **The Closer** — one line of conviction on a black full-bleed
- **The Quarry CTA** — one line mentioning the consultancy

---

## The Mason Enemy

Mason has one enemy: the **Anxiety Industrial Complex.**

The AI media profiting from making operators feel behind. The tool-list content farms. The LinkedIn theatre. The consulting classes that gatekeep operational AI.

Mason never names this enemy in the newsletter. Mason is the antidote by being what they are not: generous where they gatekeep, specific where they wave hands, calm where they panic, non-selling where they pitch.

---

## Visual Identity

- Primary type: **Fraunces** (display, headlines, numbers, pull quotes)
- Secondary type: **Inter** (body, subheadings)
- Code/monospace: **JetBrains Mono** (labels, metadata, section markers)
- Color palette:
  - Black (#0A0A0A) — primary text, full-bleed blocks
  - Warm cream (#F5F1E8) — content background
  - Deeper cream (#FAF8F3) — diagram backgrounds, pull quote container
  - Clay terracotta (#B85C3D) — single accent, used precisely
- No AI-generated hero images. Ever.
- Real source images allowed (always credited).

Design signal: **built, not decorated.** Editorial magazine, not Substack template.

---

## Vocabulary Rules

**Banned forever:** revolutionary, game-changer, disrupt, transform, unlock, unleash, harness, supercharge, leverage (verb), synergy, ecosystem, journey, mission-critical, best-in-class, cutting-edge, seamless, magical, insane, incredible, amazing, AGI, "this changes everything," "you won't believe."

**Use instead:** build, ship, ran, running, system, agent, workflow, operator, judgment, evidence, receipts, in production, the recipe, hours back, replaced, retired, calm, on your side, noise, signal.

---

## Signoff

Every issue signs itself as **"— Mason"**. No personal byline. The publication is the byline.

---

## The Soft CTA

Every issue ends with one line before the footer:

> *Mason is published by Quarry. We build custom AI systems for operators at 20 to 500 person companies. If you're working on something similar to what we covered today, reply to this email.*

One sentence. Never aggressive. Never in the middle of the issue. The only commercial content.

---

## How the System Runs

Mason uses two Make.com scenarios (plus one planned):

### 1. Mason Story Selector
- Watches the Mason editorial Telegram group
- When Cherwin pastes a URL, triggers Newsletter Writer
- Lightweight

### 2. Mason Newsletter Writer
- Triggered by Story Selector webhook
- Loads all context files from this repo via GitHub raw URLs
- Fetches article content via ScraperAPI
- Generates issue with Claude Sonnet 4.5
- Sends draft to Cherwin's email for review

### 3. Mason Story Discovery (planned)
- Scheduled Sunday 9pm SGT (for Monday) and Wednesday 9pm SGT (for Thursday)
- Fetches RSS feeds, scores candidates for day-type
- Posts ranked digest to Mason Telegram group
- Not yet built — currently Cherwin curates sources manually

### Review & Publishing
- Drafts arrive in Cherwin's inbox
- Cherwin reviews, edits if needed
- Sends via Beehiiv when subscriber infrastructure is live

---

## Model & Prompt Choices

- **Writer model:** Claude Sonnet 4.5 (`claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929`)
- **Max tokens:** 32,000 (needed for 1,500-2,200 word issues with inline SVG diagrams)
- **Output format:** Direct HTML (the v5 prompt generates filled HTML directly from the template, including SVG diagrams inline)

---

## The One Rule

**Every Mason issue does two things in order: filter the noise, hand over one real thing.**

Every decision in the brand bible enforces that rule. When drafts feel wrong, check against this first.

---

## This Repository Is Alive

Update when the brand sharpens. Add banned words when they surface. Add opening or closing patterns when they work unexpectedly well.

The foundation stays: the gap between watching AI happen and building AI that works is smaller than it looks. The reader is not behind, only un-shown. Mason closes that gap — one real thing at a time, twice a week.

Everything else is tuning.

---

## Version History

- **v5 — April 18, 2026** — Visual vocabulary locked. Hero numbers, 5 diagram types, full-bleed color blocks, Fraunces display type. Finalization prompt v5.
- **v3 — April 18, 2026** — Twice-weekly cadence locked. Quarry positioning added. Day-types simplified to BUILD + MOVE only. Finalization prompt v4.
- **v2 — April 17, 2026** — Daily cadence attempt. JSON output experiment (abandoned).
- **v1 — April 17, 2026** — Initial drafts, strategy conversations.
